perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall commence when executed and continue for a period of one (1) year.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall commence when executed and continue indefinitely, unless terminated by either party with thirty (30) days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement has a fixed term of one year, whereas the modified agreement has an indefinite term, creating an in-text contradiction about the duration of the agreement. This is now contradicting with section 2, where it specifies that the agreement will last for 1 year.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall commence when executed and continue for a period of one (1) year. The Parties agree that from the date of first receipt, and for a period of three (3) years following the last disclosure of Confidential Information, the party receiving the Confidential Information (the “Receiving Party”) shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any other person, or entity, or use for its own benefit except as provided in this Agreement and shall use the same degree of care to avoid publication or dissemination of such information as it does for its own confidential information which it does not desire to have published or disseminated. These efforts shall specifically include document control measures, such as numbered copies and sign out logs, and imposing on all employees, agents and other representatives of the Receiving Party restrictions at least as strict as required by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement term from a fixed one (1) year to an indefinite term. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which explicitly states that the agreement will continue for a period of one (1) year. The contradictory terms regarding the duration of the agreement create a strong in-text contradiction, making the agreement's term unclear and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information and any copies and extracts thereof shall be promptly returned to the disclosing party or at any time within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request by the disclosing party for the return of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information and any copies and extracts thereof shall be retained by the Receiving Party for archival purposes and shall not be returned unless explicitly requested by the Disclosing Party and approved by the Receiving Party's legal counsel.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return of Confidential Information upon request, while the modified text allows the Receiving Party to retain the information indefinitely for archival purposes unless both requested by the Disclosing party and approved by the Receiving Party. This creates an in-text contradiction about the Receiving Party's obligations regarding the handling of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information . All Confidential Information and any copies and extracts thereof shall be promptly returned to the disclosing party or at any time within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request by the disclosing party for the return of such Confidential Information. If authorized by the disclosing party, such Confidential Information may be destroyed by the Receiving Party if such destruction is certified by the Receiving Party to the satisfaction of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that confidential information shall be retained for archival purposes and not returned unless explicitly requested by the Disclosing Party and approved by the Receiving Party's legal counsel. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that 'All Confidential Information and any copies and extracts thereof shall be promptly returned to the disclosing party or at any time within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request by the disclosing party'. This meets the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because it creates conflicting obligations regarding the return of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of sharing technical, business and financial information to foster potential mutually beneficial business relationships.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall be used solely for internal research and development purposes by the Receiving Party, irrespective of whether it fosters a mutually beneficial business relationship with the Disclosing Party. This use is permitted even if it directly competes with the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts the use of Confidential Information to fostering mutually beneficial business relationships, whereas the changed text allows for internal research and development, even if competitive. This creates an in-text contradiction in terms of how the received confidential information can be used, irrespective of the original agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Limitation on Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information . Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of sharing technical, business and financial information to foster potential mutually beneficial business relationships. (a) Confidential Information shall not be copied or reproduced by the Receiving Party, except for such copies as may be reasonably required for accomplishment of the purposes stated herein; (b) Confidential Information shall be disclosed only to employees, agents, and other parties of the Receiving Party who have a “need to know” in connection with the purposes stated herein; and (c) This Agreement shall not restrict the disclosure or use of information that: (i) was in the public domain at the time of disclosure or thereafter enters the public domain through no breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (ii) was, at the time of the receipt by the Receiving Party, otherwise known to the Receiving Party without restrictions as to the use or disclosure; (iii) becomes known to the Receiving Party from a source other than the disclosing party without breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; or (iv) is developed independently by the Receiving Party and without reliance upon the Confidential Information disclosed herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text both state that Confidential Information shall be used 'solely for the purpose of sharing technical, business and financial information to foster potential mutually beneficial business relationships.' The changed text permits the use of Confidential Information for 'internal research and development purposes by the Receiving Party, irrespective of whether it fosters a mutually beneficial business relationship with the Disclosing Party.' The changed text explicitly allows for uses beyond, and even in direct competition with, the Disclosing Party. This is a direct contradiction of the original and contradicted text that limits the use to foster potential mutually beneficial business relationships only, therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Except as provided herein, the Parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by arbitration to be held in San Diego County, California, USA in accordance with the Commercial Arbitration Rules then in effect for the American Arbitration Association.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by litigation in the courts of England, in accordance with English law. Arbitration is explicitly waived.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement mandates arbitration in San Diego, California, while the changed agreement mandates litigation in England, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the dispute resolution process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Arbitration . Except as provided herein, the Parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by arbitration to be held in San Diego County, California, USA in accordance with the Commercial Arbitration Rules then in effect for the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. In the event of arbitration, the Parties may undertake a reasonable amount of discovery. The decision of the arbitrator shall be final, conclusive and binding on the parties to the arbitration. Judgement may be entered on the arbitrator’s decision in any court having jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly replaces arbitration in San Diego with litigation in England and waives arbitration, directly contradicting the contradicted text which mandates arbitration in San Diego as the dispute resolution method. The original text also enforces this arbitration agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the United States of America, State of California.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of England and Wales, without regard to conflicts of law principles. The United Nations Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods shall not apply.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement specifies California law, while the changed agreement specifies English law. This creates an in-text contradiction concerning the governing law of the agreement. This introduces an in-text contradiction with the original text of the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law . This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the United States of America, State of California.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that California law governs the agreement, which is also stated in the contradicted text. The changed text states that the laws of England and Wales govern the agreement. This directly contradicts the original clause, creating an in-text contradiction as per the explanation and aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' provided.
